instruction: The hero shot needs to be split at the halfway mark — there's a natural cut point there.
Lower the background music by 6 dB — the dialogue needs room. Also add a subtitle caption at 5s saying 'Interview begins'.
Create a new video track for the overlay layer. The B-roll footage on V2 needs to be split — cut the later overlay at the 2-second mark. And slow the hero shot to 0.8x for emphasis.
The ending credits are missing — add a caption saying 'The End' at the end of the timeline.
The title card at the beginning is outdated — remove the existing caption.
The outro segment is too long — split it at the 2-second mark. And add a saturation effect to the closing shot (intro clip).
Outro: opacity 0.9. Add lower-third text 'Thanks for watching' at 20s.
The intro-to-middle cut needs a cross-dissolve transition. And add a scene title 'Part One' at the 3-second mark.
The intro needs to pop: scale it to 1.15, add a contrast effect, and place a title card caption 'Welcome' at 0s.
The hero shot runs long — split it at the 5-second mark and add a saturation effect to the second half.
Import the new voiceover file 'narration.wav' into the project. Place a clip from it on audio track 1 starting at 5s, and link it with the intro shot.
Create a new caption track for lower thirds. Add a caption 'Interview: John Doe' on that track at 10s, then set its font size to 36.
The middle clip needs a scene break — split it at 3 seconds. Add a dissolve transition between the two halves. And put a chapter marker caption 'Part 2' at the split point.
The intro: scale to 1.1 and add a lower-third caption 'Day 1' at 2s.
The outro is too long — split it at 2 seconds and slow the second half to 0.7x for a dreamy ending.
Add a credits caption 'Directed by Jane' at 25s, and put a brightness effect on the outro clip.
Add a new audio clip from media_2 at 10s on audio track 1. Link it with the middle video shot, and lower the BGM by 3 dB.
Split the hero shot at the 4-second mark. Add a chapter title 'Act 2' at the split point, and put a saturation effect on the second half.
Create a new video track for picture-in-picture. Add a clip from media_3 on the new track at 5s, and scale the intro to 1.1.
Create a new timeline for the rough cut version. On the first timeline, slow the intro to 0.9x and add a watermark caption 'DRAFT' at 0s.
The old title caption is outdated — remove the first one. Add a fresh title 'Season 2 Premiere' at 0s. And boost the hero shot's scale to 1.1 for a more cinematic feel.
Split the middle clip at 3 seconds for a scene break. Add a dissolve transition between the two halves. Put a saturation effect on the second half and slow the outro to 0.8x.
The opening V1 clip: scale 1.05 and add a blur effect. Smooth the cut into the bridge with a wipe transition. And add a scene label 'Part 1' at 0s.
The contrast effect on the hero shot is too intense — tone it down to 0.4 intensity. Remove the later B-roll overlay entirely. Add a subtitle 'Key Moment' at 15s.
Add a title card caption 'Welcome' at 0s for the opening. Set that caption's font size to 48. Keep it in place.
Slow the hero shot to 0.85x. Now split the hero at the 5-second mark. Preserve the speed change on the first half. The second half should also keep the 0.85x speed.
Put a contrast effect on the intro. Add a lower-third caption 'Day 1' at 2s. Now lower the BGM by 5 dB. Preserve the effect and caption.
Import the new voiceover 'narration.wav' into the project. Place a clip from it on audio track 1 at 5s. Link the new audio with the intro video. Keep the import and clip.
Create a new caption track for subtitles. Add a subtitle 'Scene 1' on it at 3s. Change the subtitle's color to yellow. Preserve the track and text.
Split the hero shot at 5 seconds. Add a saturation effect to the second half. Keep the split. Add a chapter caption 'Part 2' at the split point. Preserve everything.
Add a 'Credits' caption at 25s. Slow the outro to 0.8x. Keep the caption.
Remove the outdated first caption. Add a fresh title 'Season Finale' at 0s.
Create a new video track for overlays. Add a clip from media_3 on the new track at 5s. Keep the track. Lower the BGM by 3 dB. Preserve everything.
Intro: rotation 3 degrees. Add a lower-third 'Day One' at 2s. Keep the rotation. Outro: speed 0.85x. Preserve everything.
Earlier B-roll: opacity 0.7. Later B-roll: opacity 0.55. Keep earlier change. Add a subtitle 'Overlay scene' at 10s. Preserve all.
Split the middle clip at 3 seconds. Add a saturation effect to the second half. Keep the split. Slow the outro to 0.85x. Preserve all.
Opening V1: scale 1.05. Opening V1: brightness effect. Keep scale. Add a wipe transition between the opening and bridge. Preserve all. Add a chapter title 'Introduction' at 0s. Keep everything.
Intro: scale 1.08. Split the middle clip at 4 seconds. Keep intro scale. Add a contrast effect to the second half of the split. Preserve all. Lower BGM by 4 dB. Keep everything.
The hero moment in the middle clip runs long. Split it at 3 seconds and add a dissolve transition between the two halves.
We have new background music — import 'ambient.mp3' and place a clip from it on audio track 2 starting at 0s.
The opening section needs a title card. Add a caption 'Welcome' at 0s. Make the title bigger — set its font size to 48.
I need a picture-in-picture layer. Create a new video track. Now add a clip from media_2 on the new track starting at 5s.
The hero shot has a natural cut point at 5 seconds — split it there. Add a saturation effect to the second half for a flashback look. Slow the second half to 0.75x to complete the effect.
The closing section needs credits — add a caption 'Produced by Studio X' at 25s. Change its color to white.
Create a new caption track for lower thirds, and add a subtitle 'Interview: John' on it at 10s.
The outro has a bad first half — split it at 2 seconds and delete the first part.
The title card text is wrong — remove the first caption. Add a new caption 'Episode 3: Arrival' at 0s to replace it.
Create a new audio track for sound effects. Add a clip from media_3 on the new track at 10s, and set its volume to -8 dB.
Split the middle clip at the 4-second mark and add a chapter title 'Act 2' at the split point.
Add a new audio clip from media_2 at 5s on audio track 1. Link it with the hero shot on V1. Extend the hero shot by 1 second so it covers the full audio.
One of the audio clips is stuck fully on the left — can you center it?
One of the audio clips is fully panned to the right — can you center it?
The first audio clip is stuck fully on the left — can you center it? Now the BGM is way too quiet — bring it back to normal level.
The first clip is running too fast — get it back to normal speed. Now kill that slide transition between the last two clips.
On timeline 1, the first clip has gone invisible — restore its opacity first. Now the last clip on timeline 1 looks blurry — clean that up.
Every audio clip is panned off-center — each pushed to a different side — center all of them.
The third clip is completely invisible — can you bring it back? Now scale the last video clip up to 1.1. And rotate the shortest video clip by 5 degrees — keep the opacity fix from the first turn.
The first audio clip is stuck fully on the left — can you center it? Now boost the longest audio clip by 2 dB — don't undo the pan fix.
The first audio clip is stuck fully on the left — can you center it? Now boost the BGM by 2 dB. And keep the pan fix from earlier intact.
A V2 clip is upside down — can you flip it back? Now lower the BGM by 3 dB. And add a blur effect to the last V1 clip — keep everything from before.
The first audio clip is stuck fully on the left — center it, then boost its volume by 3 dB.
The BGM is fully panned to the right — center it, then drop its volume by 3 dB.
The first audio clip is blown out loud — bring the volume back to normal, then split it at its midpoint to isolate the problem section.
The second audio clip is panned hard left and too loud — center the pan first, then set its volume to -6 dB.
The BGM is blown out loud and hard-panned left — bring the volume back to normal and center the pan, then add a fade-in and fade-out.
The last V1 clip is upside down and squeezed narrow — flip it back and restore its width, then add a saturation effect, and finally boost its matching audio clip by 3 dB.
Add a new clip from av_media_1 onto track V1 starting at 00:00:25:00.
Add a caption with text 'Hello World' at 00:00:05:00.
Split video_clip_2 at 00:00:03:00. Add a new video track named V3.
Add a cross-dissolve transition between video_clip_1 and video_clip_2 with duration 1.0s. Set video_clip_3 opacity to 0.7.
Add a new clip from av_media_2 onto track V2 at 00:00:15:00.
Split video_clip_1 at the 00:00:02:00 mark.
Split video_clip_3 at 00:00:04:00.
Add a caption 'Scene 2' at 00:00:08:00 with duration 3 seconds.
Import /assets/music.mp3 as media. Add a clip from it onto A2 at 00:00:00:00.
Unlink video_clip_2 from its linked audio. Add a caption 'Chapter 2' at 00:00:06:00.
Add a contrast effect to video_clip_4. Split video_clip_3 at 00:00:05:00. Set video_clip_1 opacity to 0.9.
Add a caption with text 'Chapter 1' at position 0.0s on video_track_1, and add a fade-in effect to video_clip_2.
video_clip_3: split at 2.5s, add brightness effect to the first part.
Create a new video track called 'overlay_track' and add a clip from av_media_2 at position 0.0s with duration 3.0s on it.
Add a cross-dissolve transition between vc_1 and vc_2 with duration 1.0s, and set vc_2 opacity to 0.95.
Remove caption_1 and add a new caption with text 'Scene 2' at position 5.0s.
Remove video_clip_2 and add a new clip from av_media_3 at position 2.0s with duration 4.0s on video_track_1.
Add a new audio track called 'sfx_track', add a clip from av_media_2 at 1.0s with duration 2.0s on it, and add a fade-in effect to that new clip.
Split vc_2 at 3.0s, set the first annotator_b's opacity to 0.8, and add a cross-dissolve transition between the two resulting parts.
Remove video_clip_2 and video_clip_4, then add a new clip from av_media_1 at position 3.0s with duration 5.0s on video_track_1.
Add a caption 'Title Card' at 0.0s, set video_clip_1 opacity to 0.8, and set video_clip_3 scale to 0.9.
Import 'overlay.png' as new media, add a clip from it at 1.0s with duration 2.0s on video_track_1, and add a fade-out effect to the new clip.
Remove audio_track_2, create a new audio track called 'voiceover', and add a clip from av_media_1 at 0.0s with duration 10.0s on it.
Add a cross-dissolve transition between vc_1 and vc_2 lasting 1.2s, add a saturation effect to vc_2, and set vc_2 scale to 0.95.
Split video_clip_5 at 4.0s. Add a blur effect to the first part. Add a cross-dissolve transition between the two resulting segments. Set the second segment's opacity to 0.7.

Import 'title_bg.mp4' as media. Create a new video track called 'title_track'. Add a clip from the imported media at 0.0s with duration 5.0s. Add a caption 'Welcome' at 0.5s. Add a fade-in effect to the new clip.
Unlink video_clip_2 from audio_clip_2. Remove video_clip_2. Remove audio_clip_2. Add a new video clip from av_media_1 at 2.0s with duration 3.0s on video_track_1. Link the new clip with audio_clip_4.

Create a bin called 'Assets', import 'interview.mp4', add a clip from it at 5.0s with duration 8.0s on video_track_1, add a brightness effect to the clip, set the clip's scale to 0.9, and add a caption 'Interview' at 5.0s.

Remove the transition between vc_1 and vc_2 and add a wipe transition with duration 1.5s between them. Set vc_2 rotation to 12 degrees, add a contrast effect to vc_1, and add a caption 'Opening' at 0s.

Create a video track 'composite', add a clip from bin_media at 0.0s for 3.0s, and add another clip from bin_media at 3.0s for 4.0s on it. Link the first new clip with ac_1, add a cross-dissolve transition between the two new clips with duration 0.5s, and add a blur effect to the second new clip.
Add a caption 'Intro' at timeline 0s with duration 3 seconds. Change the caption text to 'Introduction' and set font size to 32. Keep it at 0s.
Split video_clip_2 at the 3-second mark. Set the first half's speed to 1.5x. Keep the split intact. Add a saturation effect to the second half. Preserve the speed change.
Create a new video track above the existing ones. Add a clip from av_media_1 on the new track at 0s. Keep the track.
Add a caption 'Breaking' at 5s with duration 3 seconds. Set the caption's font size to 48 for emphasis. Keep the caption. Slow video_clip_2 to 0.5x to match the dramatic moment. Preserve everything.
Add a caption 'Scene 1' at 0s. Set its color to yellow. Keep the text.
Remove caption_1. Add a new caption 'Updated Title' at the same position. Keep the removal.
Split video_clip_3 at 4 seconds. Add a contrast effect to the first half. Preserve the split.
Import 'logo.png' as media. Add it as a clip at 0s on the top video track. Scale it to 0.25 for a watermark. Keep the import and placement.
Create a new video track. Add a clip from av_media_2 on the new track at 2s. Keep the track. Apply a blur effect to the new clip. Preserve everything.
Add a caption 'Chapter 2' at 30s. Set font size to 48 and color to white. Apply a fade-in effect to the caption. Preserve all changes.
Split video_clip_3 at the midpoint. Add a cross-dissolve transition between the two halves. Keep the split.
Unlink video_clip_3 from audio_clip_3. Remove video_clip_3. Keep the unlink intact. Add a caption 'Cut here' at the position where video_clip_3 was. Preserve prior.
Import 'narration.wav' as media. Add it as an audio clip at 0s. Link the new audio with video_clip_1. Keep all prior.

Add a caption 'Act II' at 15s. Apply a fade-in effect to the caption. Keep the caption. Speed video_clip_2 to 1.5x. Preserve the caption and effect. Add a cross-dissolve between video_clip_1 and video_clip_2. Preserve everything.

Split video_clip_1 at 3 seconds. Add a blur effect to the second half. Keep the split. Add a cross-dissolve transition between the two halves. Preserve the effect. Slow the second half to 0.75x. Preserve everything.

Create a new audio track named 'SFX'. Import 'explosion.wav' as media. Keep the track. Add the imported audio as a clip at 10s on the SFX track. Preserve all. Link the SFX clip with video_clip_3. Keep everything intact.

Unlink vc_2 from ac_2. Split vc_2 at 3 seconds. Keep the unlink. Remove the first half. Preserve the split's second piece. Add a saturation effect to the remaining half and re-link it with ac_2. Preserve all.
Split video_clip_2 at the 3-second mark and slow the second half to 0.5x.
Add a caption saying 'Chapter 1' at timeline 0s. Change its font size to 48 and color to white.
Add a new audio clip from av_media_2 starting at 5s on audio track 1. Link the new audio clip with video_clip_2. Extend video_clip_2 by 1 second so it covers the full audio.
Split video_clip_3 at the midpoint. Add a saturation effect to the second half. Insert a cross-dissolve transition between the two halves.
Import 'overlay.png' as media and add it as a clip at 0s on video track 1.
Split video_clip_1 at the 2-second mark and delete the first half.
Add a caption 'Breaking News' at 10s and set its font size to 48 with a red color for emphasis.
Create a new caption track and place a subtitle 'Scene 2' on it at 5s.
Remove caption_1 and add a replacement caption 'Revised Title' at the same position.
Split video_clip_2 at 4 seconds. Speed up the first half to 1.5x and slow the second half to 0.75x.
Import 'logo.png' as new media. Add it as a clip at 0s and scale it to 0.3 for a watermark.
Add a caption 'Interview' at 0s. Set its font to bold, size 36, then apply a fade-in effect to it.
Split video_clip_5 at the 3-second mark. Add a cross-dissolve transition between the two resulting halves.
Add a 3-second clip from av_media_1 at the start of the timeline. Apply a blur effect to it and set its opacity to 0.6 as a background.
Unlink video_clip_1 from audio_clip_1. Split video_clip_1 at 3 seconds and delete the first half. Re-link the remaining half with audio_clip_1.
Create a new video track. Add a title card clip from av_media_1 on it at 0s. Add a caption 'Episode 1' over the title card and link them.
Split the longest video clip on the timeline at its midpoint.
Split the longest video clip on V1 at its midpoint. Remove the shorter caption from the timeline. Lower the BGM volume by 3 dB.
Add a blur effect to the last video clip on the main video track.
Split the shortest audio clip on track 1 at its midpoint.
Remove the video clip that starts last on the timeline.
Unlink the video clip and audio clip that are linked together at the start of the timeline.
Set the longest video's rotation to 3 degrees. Split the shortest audio clip on track 1 at its midpoint.
Add a caption 'Highlight' at the start time of the longest video clip. Mute the background music audio clip.
Set the duration of the transition closest to the start of the timeline to 2 seconds. Lower the volume of the second audio clip by 4 dB.
Unlink the video and audio clips that are linked at the end of the timeline. Set the longest video clip's speed to 0.8x.
Remove the transition nearest the end of the timeline. Add a 2-second black clip after the last video clip on V1. Lower the second audio clip's volume by 3 dB.
Import a media file called 'outro.mp4' into the bin. Add a caption 'Credits' at the timeline position where the longest V1 clip ends. Remove the shortest caption currently on the timeline.
Split the shortest video clip at its midpoint and add a blur effect to the first half.
On the last clip on the timeline, remove any existing blur effect and add a brightness effect instead.
Split the longest audio clip at the 10-second mark and mute the second half.
Split the longest video at its midpoint and add a fade-out to the first half. Add a cross-dissolve transition between the two resulting halves.
Remove the shortest caption and add a new caption 'Updated' at the same position. Set the longest V1 clip's speed to 0.9x and opacity to 0.85.
On the first video on the timeline, set x to 20 and add a contrast effect. On the last video, set y to -10 and add a brightness effect.
Add a caption 'Intro' at the start of the first video clip and set it to 2 seconds long. Add a caption 'Outro' at the start of the last video clip and set it to 2 seconds long.

Split the longest video at its midpoint and add transitions between the halves. On the BGM, set volume to -5 dB and add a fade-in effect. Add a caption 'Part 1' over the first half and 'Part 2' over the second half.
On the first V1 clip, add a fade-in and set opacity 0.95. On the last V1 clip, add a fade-out and set opacity 0.95. Remove the longer V2 clip and update the remaining V2 clip's scale to 1.3.

On timeline 1, mute the audio clip that starts earliest. Now increase the playback speed of the video clip that ends latest to 1.5x. Preserve the mute. Split the longest video clip at its midpoint, keeping the earlier two changes intact. Finally, add a fade-in to the first half of the split. Don't undo anything from before.

Link the first video clip with the audio clip that plays at the same time. Then add a blur effect to the last video clip on V1. Also mute the audio clip that starts at the same time as the first video clip.
Add a contrast effect to the last video clip on V1. Boost the volume of the audio that plays at the same time by 3 dB.
Split the longest video clip at its midpoint. Then add a blur effect to the first half.
Find the video clip with the longest duration and add a fade-out effect. Also set its opacity to 0.85.
Remove the last video clip on the timeline. Also unlink the first video and audio pair. Keep the removal.
Set the opacity of the video clip that starts last on V1 to 0.6. Also mute the audio clip that overlaps with it.
Speed up the longest video clip to 1.25x. Now mute the audio that plays while it plays. Then add a caption 'Highlight' at the timeline position where it starts.
Mute the audio clip with the longest duration. Then reduce the volume of the second-longest audio clip to -10 dB.
Longest audio: -3 dB. First audio on track 1: add a fade-in effect. Last video on V1: rotate 5 degrees. Keep earlier changes.
Set the first transition's duration to 2 seconds. Now remove it and add a fade-out effect to the clip before it instead. Keep any prior state.

On timeline 1, find the video clip with the longest duration and scale it to 0.85. Now set the quietest audio clip's volume to 0 dB. Rotate the scaled clip by 5 degrees. Preserve everything. Finally add a saturation effect to the earliest-starting video clip.

On timeline 1, find the video clip that starts earliest and reduce its opacity to 0.8. Next, find the video clip that starts latest and mute the audio that plays with it. Now add a caption 'End' at the position of the latest-starting clip. Preserve earlier changes.
Split the longest clip on V1 at its midpoint. Add a cross-dissolve transition between the two resulting halves.
Mute the longest audio clip (the BGM). Raise the volume of the longest V1 clip's audio by 4 dB to compensate, and add a caption 'No BGM' at its start.
Remove the transition closest to the end of the timeline and add a fade-out effect to the clip before it.
Split the longest V1 clip at its midpoint. Add a caption 'Part 2' over the second half of the split.
Add a fade-in to the first V1 clip. And a fade-out to the last V1 clip so the sequence has clean bookends.
Remove the last video clip on the timeline. Update the caption 'End' to match the new last clip's end time.
Remove the transition between the intro and second clip on V1. Add a 1-second gap between those clips by shifting the later one forward.
On timeline 1, speed up the middle V1 clip to 1.5x. Then shift the last audio clip on timeline 1 earlier to match the new timing.
Split the longest V1 clip at its midpoint. Remove the second half since it has poor footage. Add a fade-out to the remaining first half.
On timeline 1, speed up the middle V1 clip to 2x. Shift the clip after it on timeline 1 earlier to close the gap. Also shift the last audio clip on timeline 1 earlier by the saved time.
